async-keyword.rationale: `async`/`await` is the sugar that hides the erased error channel of `Promise<T>`.

promise-type.rationale: `Promise<T>` erases the error channel; tagged-error or `Effect<T, E, R>` keep it visible.

then-chain.rationale: `.then()` chains hide the error channel and reorder control flow; same erasure as `Promise<T>`.

bare-catch.rationale: A silent catch hides both the error AND the branch; exhaustiveness cannot apply.

no-conditional-chaining.rationale: Conditional chains (`x?.y?.z`) hide the optionality the type system would have made exhaustive; lift the absence into a discriminant.

no-unbounded-concurrency.rationale: Unbounded `Promise.all` over a runtime-sized input has no concurrency budget; bound it explicitly.

record-cast.rationale: `as Record<string, unknown>` papers over a missing schema at the boundary; decode instead.

no-raw-sql.rationale: Raw SQL defeats the compiler; a typed builder makes the schema load-bearing at compile time.

no-manual-enum-cast.rationale: Hand-written unions drift from their source; generated or schema-derived enums do not.

no-vitest-mocks.rationale: An integration test that mocks the boundary asserts your code works against your mock, not the real thing.

no-hardcoded-secrets.rationale: A hardcoded secret bypasses the env-boundary schema; secrets must be decoded at boot, not embedded in source.

no-raw-throw-new-error.rationale: `throw new Error("bad")` has no type, no handling contract, no receipt for the caller; tagged errors do.

as-unknown-as.rationale: `as unknown as T` is a double-cast that bypasses the type system at the boundary; decode through a schema.

no-process-env-at-runtime.rationale: Reading `process.env.X` outside boot bypasses the env schema; environment is a boundary, validated at boot.

no-test-skip-only.rationale: Test-hygiene corollary of principle 1: a `.skip` or `.only` shipped to main is a test that does not test.

no-coverage-threshold-gate.rationale: Coverage % is a diagnostic, never a CI gate (Inozemtseva & Holmes ICSE 2014). Acts on the wrong signal.

no-hardcoded-assertion-literals.rationale: Assertions hardcoding a literal (e.g., `expect(...).toBe(42)` where 42 is the implementation's output) compress the same information as a property; the property has higher coverage.

no-example-only-tests.rationale: A test that asserts one hand-picked input is a compression of a property the type system or `fast-check` could verify; promote to property when one exists.

effect-error-erasure.rationale: `Effect<T, never, R>` claims no error channel; if the effect can fail, name the error tag, don't erase it.

effect-promise.rationale: `Promise<T>` inside an `Effect` re-introduces the erased error channel; convert at the boundary.

either-discriminant.rationale: `Either<L, R>` matches must discriminate on the tag, never on truthiness; truthy-checking conflates error with falsy data.

no-effect-error-coalescing.rationale: Coalescing distinct effect errors into one tag erases the diagnostic; downstream needs the tag set the upstream produced.

tag-discriminant.rationale: Discriminated unions match on the named tag, not on shape; shape-matching breaks when tags overlap.

manual-brand.rationale: A hand-rolled brand without a smart constructor enforces nothing; use the project's `Brand` helper so the constructor is the only path in.

manual-option.rationale: Hand-rolled `Option`-shaped types don't compose with the rest of the algebra; use the project's `Option` so `match` works uniformly.

manual-result.rationale: Hand-rolled `Result`-shaped types miss the discriminant guarantees; use the project's `Result` so error tags stay structural.

manual-tagged-error.rationale: Hand-rolled tagged errors miss the constructor pattern that makes the tag set extensible; use the project's helper.

no-exported-brand-constructor.rationale: Exporting a brand's smart constructor breaks the encapsulation that makes the brand load-bearing; the constructor stays private to the module that owns the validation.

no-manual-brand-constructor.rationale: Manually constructing a branded value bypasses the validation that earned the brand; use the smart constructor.

file-implicit-boundary-module.rationale: Single-file modules must declare boundary intent; the architect's design surface is explicit, not inferred.

folder-explicit-api-required.rationale: Folders with two or more consumers must expose a curated `index.ts` (Facade pattern); implicit APIs let consumers reach past the boundary.

folder-readme-required.rationale: Each folder of structural significance carries a README naming its intent; the architect's design lives next to the code.

no-cross-domain-sibling-import.rationale: Sibling-folder imports across domains pierce the architect's boundaries; cross-domain code routes through a declared shared kernel, not a sideways import.

no-distant-folder-import.rationale: Long import paths reverse the cohesion the folder structure declared; locality of import = locality of concern.

no-export-star-boundary.rationale: `export *` at a public boundary inventories instead of curating; the architect's facade is intentional, not exhaustive.

no-folder-cycle.rationale: Folder dependency cycles invert the architect's design intent; cycles signal a missing kernel module or a misplaced concern.

no-implementation-file-public-entry.rationale: Public entrypoints belong in `index.ts`, not arbitrary implementation files; the package's public surface is one named door.

no-internal-subpath-export.rationale: `package.json` exports must not expose `src/`, `internal/`, or test helpers; internal paths are not contracts.

no-inventory-barrel.rationale: Index files curate exports rather than inventory all siblings; bulk re-export defeats encapsulation.

no-large-folder.rationale: Folders past a configurable file-count threshold are doing two jobs and need to split; cohesion has a budget.

no-large-public-surface.rationale: Public re-export fanout has a budget; over-budget facades signal an over-scoped package.

no-package-mesh.rationale: Package dependencies form a DAG, never a mesh; mesh dependencies kill independent reasoning.

no-public-infra-type-leak.rationale: Infrastructure package names in public boundaries leak the dependency through the API; adapt at the boundary.

no-public-test-helper-leak.rationale: Test fixtures and helpers stay private to the package; leaking them publicly conflates test contract with runtime contract.

no-public-vendor-type-leak.rationale: Public types referencing vendor packages (Kysely, Pino, OpenAI) leak the dependency through the API; the Adapter pattern owns this seam.

no-root-internal-cycle.rationale: Public exports must not import private internals; the public/internal split is one-way.

no-upward-layer-import.rationale: Layered architecture forbids upward imports; the lower layer cannot depend on the upper, ever.

require-boundary-owned-types.rationale: Public exports use package-owned type names, not imported foreign names; boundaries own their own vocabulary.

require-curated-public-facade.rationale: Public exports come from an explicit re-export strategy; the facade is designed, not auto-generated.

shared-kernel-cohesion.rationale: Shared folders must not depend on consuming domain folders; the kernel sits below its consumers, never alongside.

architecture-directive-parse-error.rationale: An `@agent-code-guard/architecture-exception:` directive without a `reason:` line is a silent override; exceptions to architectural rules must carry their reasoning.

require-knip-in-lint.rationale: Dead-code detection (`knip`) running in lint catches drifted exports before they accumulate; the linter checks structure, not just syntax.
